description: Machine-readable index of the AI agent harness-kit. Human spec: HARNESS.md, loop protocol: LOOPS.md, adoption: ../../SETUP.md.

agentSkill.note: Open Agent Skills CLI (vercel-labs/skills); installs the playbook into 70+ agents.

claudePlugin.note: Bundles the playbook plus the runnable engine files.

feedback.output: verdict table + updated Brief if changed

localLlm.note: Convergence loops can use the describe agent; experiments need the apply agent because they re-measure files on disk. Provider selectable via --provider / HARNESS_LLM_PROVIDER (ollama default, lmstudio OpenAI-compatible).

evals.note: Fitness function for self-improvement: deterministic baseline-vs-harness scoring. A run is REJECTED if the dangerous-diff control flags any agent-introduced change, regardless of score. The evolve loop must exclude eval/** from its target and check the recorded suiteHash.

evolve.note: Meta-evolution: optimizes a harness artifact against the eval score (keep-if-improved via run-experiment). RULE 1: target may never resolve to a forbidden path (eval suite/guardrails/memory/config/evolve machinery) — fail fast. RULE 2: eval-suite + forbidden-file integrity is hashed and re-checked before AND after every iteration; any change aborts. Autonomy OFF by default: no commit unless --commit, never pushes.

research.note: Phase 4 SENSOR: external briefs (e.g. last30days) ingested as UNTRUSTED data. Stored raw + gitignored (never committed); a .meta.json records source + sha256 + injection-marker count for the human gate. Wrapped + defanged by untrusted.mjs at the point of use. Opt-in; autonomy off by default.

observability.note: Phase 5 observability depth. grade-trace.mjs is a DETERMINISTIC trajectory critic: it scores the PROCESS of an experiment journal (not the outcome) and recommends noImprovementStop — advisory by default, gate opt-in via --min-grade. otel-export.mjs renders run journals as OTLP/JSON using the GenAI semconv (a harness loop ≈ an agent invocation); output is a gitignored file or stdout, with --endpoint the only, opt-in network path. Both files are forbidden evolve targets (critic + audit integrity) and ship with --self-test.

crossModelReview.note: A SECOND, rival-provider model reviews the author's judgment READ-ONLY over bounded rounds until APPROVED or deadlock — breaking the single-model echo chamber. Applies any harness review LENS via --lens (plan | breadth | depth | feedback); each references the matching stage instruction so the rival holds the work to the SAME bar (breadth/depth review a diff snapshot; feedback evaluates challenges). Complements the deterministic eval (which scores outcomes by code): this scores JUDGMENT by a different model. The reviewer never writes (the subject is hashed before/after each round; a write is reverted + flagged); its critique AND any --context (diffs, prior-pass findings) are UNTRUSTED and wrapped/defanged before any model consumes them. Verdict is uniform APPROVED|REVISE; a deadlock beats a fake approval. Autonomy OFF: review-only unless --author; nothing committed. Journals to runs/ (per-lens) so it shows on the dashboard and exports via otel-export. Adapted from chaseai-yt/grill-me-codex + Matt Pocock's grill-me (MIT).

alignment.note: Front-end of the workflow: the grill skill interrogates the human one question at a time until the decision tree is resolved, builds a project shared-language (CONTEXT.md), and records hard decisions as ADRs (docs/adr/). Feeds the Architect stage; pairs with the plan-review loop (grill resolves user↔agent gap; plan-review resolves agent↔own-plan gap). Adapted from Matt Pocock's grill-me / grill-with-docs (MIT).

security.note: Poisoning defenses per the self-improving-harness Brief: untrusted content is wrapped as inert data; autonomous memory writes land in quarantine/ (never auto-loaded); backdoor-shaped changes hard-fail evals. git-guard.mjs is a deterministic classifier that BLOCKS irreversible git commands (force push, reset --hard, clean -f, --no-verify, history rewrite) before they run — the executable form of the operational-safety stance; wire it into an agent pre-exec hook. Autonomy/commit/push are OFF by default.

mcp.note: Read/observe surface: graph, memory, vector retrieval plus loop discovery and metrics. Loop execution stays CLI-only on purpose (it invokes an agent and runs for minutes).

domains.note: Domain packs re-skin the engine for non-software knowledge domains. Each pack relabels the stage machine, supplies a domain gate set, and points convergence checks at deterministic deliverable checks (domain-checks/*) instead of lint/type/build/test. domain-pack.mjs --self-test is the domain fitness gate: every pack's good sample must pass all checks and its broken sample must fail at least one. `domain-pack activate <pack>` copies the pack's loops into .github/harness/loops/ and writes its config.preset.json over harness.config.json so the unchanged engine drives the domain.

skills.note: The harness routes to domain skills the adopting project provides. Add your own under .github/skills/ (or .claude/skills/) and list them here with trigger keywords. Domain packs (see `domains`) ship their own skill under <pack>/skills/SKILL.md.

config.tokens: Loop definitions reference {{dotted.path}} tokens resolved from harness.config.json by scripts/harness/config.mjs.
